QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_21

question.text: A client has a net open position of USD 18 million in USDINR futures. The Extreme Loss Margin (ELM) rate for USDINR is 1%. What is the ELM amount the clearing corporation will levy on this position?

answer.text: Rs 18 lakh (USD 18 million x 1% = USD 180,000, which at notional INR conversion equals 1% of the notional value)
Rs 9 lakh, because ELM for currency is 0.50% for all pairs
Rs 36 lakh, because ELM is 2% for currency derivatives similar to equity index futures
Rs 63 lakh, because ELM is 3.5% for all exchange-traded derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELM for currency derivatives is 1% of the notional value for USDINR pairs and 0.50% for cross-currency pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY). On USD 18 million notional, ELM = 1% x 18,000,000 = USD 180,000 equivalent in INR. **The Trap:** The 0.50% rate applies only to cross-currency pairs (non-INR), not to USDINR. The 2% and 3.5% rates are equity segment ELM rates for index and stock derivatives respectively. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compare ELM rates across segments — Currency: 1% USDINR / 0.50% cross; Equity: 2% index / 3.5% stocks. Interest rate derivatives use SPAN-based margining similar to equity. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELM is charged over and above SPAN margin. Lower ELM for cross-currency pairs reflects their lower volatility compared to USDINR pairs.
